question: Does the output include exactly two images as requested?
Is the content presented in an interleaved format containing both images and text?
Do the images appear in the specific order requested: 'flat lighting' first, followed by 'Rembrandt lighting'?
Does the first image depict a realistic, front-facing white-haired elderly man?
Does the first image demonstrate 'flat lighting' (broad flat lighting), characterized by even illumination and minimal shadows across the face?
Does the second image depict a realistic, front-facing white-haired elderly man?
Does the second image demonstrate 'Rembrandt lighting', characterized by a distinct light/shadow split and a key light positioned to the side?
Is a distinct 'inverted triangle' of light visible on the shadowed cheek of the subject in the second image?
In the second image, does the shadow from the nose visually connect with the cheek shadow to define/enclose the illuminated triangle?
Are both generated images photorealistic and free of severe AI artifacts (e.g., facial distortion, asymmetry, malformed features)?
Do both images depict the recognizable same identity (consistent facial features, hair, and age) across the two lighting setups?
Is the subject's pose and head angle consistent between the two images (both front-facing)?
Does the text correctly define 'Rembrandt lighting' as a directional setup (e.g., light ~45 degrees to side/above) that creates specific shadow patterns?
Does the text explicitly explain that the 'inverted triangle' light patch is located on the cheek of the shadowed side of the face (under the eye)?
Does the text explicitly explain the formation cause of the triangle (specifically mentioning the shadow of the nose connecting with the shadow of the cheek/jaw)?
Does the text description of the shadow patterns accurately match the visual evidence shown in the generated Rembrandt image (e.g., correct side, correct triangle placement)?
Does the output demonstrate precise lighting control by showing a stark, recognizable contrast between the flat and Rembrandt lighting styles on the same subject?
